channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Girls Soccer: Bonita Vista 2, Torrey Pines 1
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- CIF Girls Open Division semifinals Bonita Vista Barons on track to repeat if with a win playing at Torrey Pines on Wednesday night. Falcons lead 1-0, but then Bonita Vista scored on a penalty kick that Ava Gavlan ripped to the net to tie the match at 1.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Basketball: Carlsbad 73, Cathedral 61
item.description:
Carlsbad basketball had a repeat on its mind: a goal to get back to the Open Division title game.
Consider it mission accomplished after a 73 to 61 win over a hard charging Cathedral team.
Jett Kenady was the difference for the Lancers on Tuesday night, scoring 24 points. Jake Hall added 22.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz lose to Kings on Kessler's big night, 118-101
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Despite a season-high 25 points and 14 points from Walker Kessler, the Utah Jazz lost to the Sacramento Kings Wednesday night at the Delta Center, 118-101. Keegan Murray scored a season-high 26 points and Zach Levine added 22 to lift the hot-shooting Kings.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: No. 7 St. John’s tops Butler 76-70 to clinch share of 1st Big East regular-season title in 33 years
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — RJ Luis Jr. scored 24 points and No. 7 St. John’s clinched a share of its first Big East regular-season title in 33 years by beating Butler 76-70 on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS GOFF, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Shaedon Sharpe makes a case for the best dunk in the NBA this season
item.description: Shaedon Sharpe has been one of the NBA’s most gifted young dunk artists since he was selected seventh overall by the Portland Trail Blazers in the 2022 NBA draft.
item.dc:creator: Ian Nicholas Quillen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Saints GM and coach indicate they plan to stick with QB Derek Carr
item.description: New Orleans Saints general manager Mickey Loomis says he sees 11-year veteran quarterback Derek Carr as someone the Saints can win with and a player the club remains “excited about.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Riley Gaines sends warning to Democrats, NCAA during national battle over trans athletes in women's sports
item.description: Former NCAA swimmer and OutKick host Riley Gaines warned Democrats and the NCAA Wednesday about multiple conflicts involving transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sen Tommy Tuberville calls Maine gov a 'total a--' for defying Trump on trans athletes
item.description: Sen. Tommy Tuberville, R-Ala., called out Maine Gov. Janet Mills over her recent feud with President Donald Trump and her refusal to ban trans athletes from girls' sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shohei Ohtani to make spring training debut against Angels on Friday night, Roberts says
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Dodgers manager Dave Roberts says three-time MVP Shohei Ohtani will make his first spring training appearance of the year on Friday night against his old team, the Los Angeles Angels. Ohtani, 30, will be the designated hitter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rob Gronkowski addresses rumored NFL return as report links him to Broncos
item.description: Former start tight end Rob Gronkowski addressed rumors Wednesday that he was mulling a possible return to the NFL after one report linked him to the Denver Broncos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Women's basketball team forfeits multiple games after not traveling to Los Angeles amid wildfire crisis
item.description: Northwestern's women's basketball team has been dealt two losses for not traveling to LA during the wildfires for games against USC and UCLA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maine lawmaker censured for posting picture of trans student on Facebook
item.description: (NewsNation) — A state representative in Maine was censured by other members of the legislature after posting a picture of a trans teen girl on her public Facebook page. The resolution passed 75-70 in a party-line vote, the Portland Press-Herald reported. Per Maine House rules, Republican Rep.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: New Orleans Privateers player suspensions related to possible gambling violations, AP source says
item.description: Four New Orleans players who’ve been suspended since late January are under investigation for possible NCAA violations related to sports gambling, a person familiar with the situation said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wrestling at Frontwave Arena: a preview with Thunder Rosa
item.description: All Elite Wrestling (AEW) is set to bring an action-packed night to Oceanside's Frontwave Arena on Wednesday, with stars like Thunder Rosa, a trailblazer in women's wrestling, ready to take center stage.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Vanderbilt guard Mikayla Blakes eager to grow thanks to attention from her record-setting play
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Vanderbilt freshman Mikayla Blakes knows each time she drops the ball in the basket it will bring more attention. That's what happens when someone scores 53 points, then sets the NCAA freshman single-game scoring record with 55 just 17 days later.
item.dc:creator: TERESA M. WALKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Matildas condemn misogynistic comments by radio host about Australian women's soccer team
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — The Matildas joined in the heavy criticism Wednesday of a commercial radio host’s misogynistic and crass comments about Australia’s national women’s soccer team. The host has since been taken off-air.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GJ River Hawks 19U girls team set for first-ever playoffs at River City Sportplex
item.description: River City Sportplex is currently in the midst of the first-ever GJ River Hawks’ 19U Girls hockey season. Now, it's playoff time.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Japanese forward 'King Kazu' turns 58 and prepares for his 40th season in professional soccer
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese forward Kazuyoshi Miura turned 58 on Wednesday. Happy Birthday to the player known as “King Kazu.” But there's more to celebrate. He's also set to start his 40th season in professional soccer, the oldest player in the Japanese Football League.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia recovers from No. 3 Florida's comeback and defeat Gators 88-83 despite Richard's 30 points
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Georgia guard Blue Cain sank a go-ahead 3-pointer with 47 seconds remaining after No. 3 Florida took its first lead by scoring 13 unanswered points, and the Bulldogs beat the Gators 88-83 on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chicago Bears NFL Scouting Combine Preview: Bears picks, workout schedule and more
item.description: The Chicago Bears have seven picks in the 2025 NFL Draft, including five picks in the first five rounds, and are looking to address needs in the offensive line, defensive line, running back, tight end, and safety positions.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz late comeback falls short against Portland
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Anfernee Simons had 28 points and out six assists to lead the Portland Trail Blazers to a 114-112 victory over the Utah Jazz on Monday night. Keyonte George, who scored 21 points, missed a 25-foot three-pointer at the buzzer that would have won the game.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Giddey scores 25 points as the Bulls snap a 6-game skid with a 142-110 rout of the 76ers
item.description: Josh Giddey had 25 points, 16 rebounds and six assists as the Chicago Bulls ended a six-game skid by routing the Philadelphia 76ers 142-110 on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: University of Utah fires head basketball coach Craig Smith
item.description: The University of Utah has fired the head coach of its men's basketball program, Craig Smith. Assistant coach Josh Eilert will take over for the remainder of the season.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Robertson scores 2 goals in the 2nd period as the Maple Leafs beat the Blackhawks 5-2
item.description: Nicholas Robertson scored twice in the second period as the Toronto Maple Leafs beat the Chicago Blackhawks 5-2 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: San Diego FC wins inaugural match against LA Galaxy 2-0
item.description: San Diego FC dominated its inaugural match against LA Galaxy 2-0 in the 30th MLS season, scoring its first-ever goal in the 52nd minute.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago Sky acquire 2-time All-Star Atkins from Washington Mystics for 2025 No. 3 pick
item.description: The Chicago Sky have traded their top pick in the upcoming WNBA draft for two-time All-Star guard Ariel Atkins from the Washington Mystics.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: University of Arizona apologizes for offensive chants aimed at BYU basketball team
item.description: The University of Arizona Athletics Department is apologizing after a derogatory chant aimed at BYU players ensued at a men's basketball game Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Keyonte George scores 30 in Jazz win over Rockets
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Keyonte George scored 30 points and Lauri Markkanen added 23 points and 10 rebounds to rally the Utah Jazz to a 124-115 victory over the Houston Rockets on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Lakers snap Nuggets' 9-game winning streak behind Luka Doncic's best game yet for LA
item.description: Luka Doncic scored 32 points in his best game by far with the Lakers, leading Los Angeles to a 123-100 runaway win at Denver on Saturday night that snapped the Nuggets' nine-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, Fox 5
